compiled_state.title: Dedicated RED Test Writer Sub-Agent with Escalation Protocol
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
The v2-v3 ticket system migration post-mortem revealed that RED test phases produce change-detector tests (grepping source files for patterns) instead of behavioral assertions that verify code actually works. Skills testing is a major gap — current tests do little more than grep skills for instructions. These weaknesses allowed fundamental problems (non-functional Jira bridge, incomplete v2 reference cleanup) to pass through TDD gates undetected.

This epic creates a specialized RED test writer sub-agent that enforces behavioral test creation, plus a skills eval framework that behaviorally validates skills operating as expected. The agent would be added to the routing logic used by other skills (sprint, fix-bug, tdd-workflow) to select an appropriate sub-agent for RED test phases.

## Success Criteria
(Placeholder — to be refined via /dso:brainstorm)

1. A dedicated dso:red-test-writer plugin agent that writes behavioral RED tests, not change-detector tests
2. The agent enforces the behavioral test requirement from implementation-plan: tests must execute code under test and assert on output/exit code/side effects, not grep source files
3. The agent is integrated into routing logic (discover-agents.sh or equivalent) so skills can dispatch it for RED test phases
4. A skills eval framework that goes beyond grep-based instruction checks — evals invoke skills (or representative subsets) and assert observable outcomes
5. Eval creation is enforced during RED test phases for skill changes

## Related Tickets
- dso-oo34: Dedicated RED test writer sub-agent (existing epic — subsume)
- 9c16-7780: TDD RED phase produces change-detector tests instead of behavioral assertions (existing bug — addresses root cause)
- bbba-8154: Planning Resilience epic (parent post-mortem; this epic was broken out from item 7)

## Approach
(Placeholder) Specialized sub-agent with strong prompt guidance on behavioral vs. structural test patterns. Routing integration via agent-routing.conf or discover-agents.sh. Skills eval framework extends existing test infrastructure with skill invocation harness and behavioral assertions.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
Developers using DSO's TDD workflows (sprint, fix-bug) cannot trust RED phase results because sub-agents frequently produce change-detector tests — tests that grep source files for patterns rather than executing code and asserting on behavioral outcomes. In the v2-v3 ticket system migration (bbba-8154), non-functional code passed TDD gates undetected because RED tests checked for string patterns in source files rather than exercising the code. This epic creates a specialized RED test writer agent that enforces behavioral test creation, with a three-tier escalation protocol that handles edge cases where behavioral testing is infeasible.

## Success Criteria
1. A `dso:red-test-writer` plugin agent (sonnet) that receives atomic RED tasks from implementation-plan and writes behavioral tests — tests that execute code under test and assert on output, exit code, or side effects
2. The agent rejects change-detector patterns (grepping source files, checking file contents for string patterns) and produces a diagnostic failure message when the task spec is insufficient to write a behavioral test
3. A `dso:red-test-evaluator` plugin agent (opus) that receives RED agent failures and returns a structured verdict: REVISE (with impact assessment of in-progress and closed tasks; max one REVISE per task before user escalation), REJECT (retry at opus tier), or CONFIRM (TDD infeasible — infrastructure, injection, documentation, or reference removal tasks)
4. Sprint and fix-bug orchestrators dispatch RED test tasks via direct `subagent_type: "dso:red-test-writer"` and handle the three-tier escalation protocol: sonnet attempt → opus evaluation on failure (REVISE/REJECT/CONFIRM) → opus retry on REJECT → user escalation on retry failure or second REVISE failure

## Dependencies
None — subsumes dso-oo34. Addresses root cause of 9c16-7780.

## Approach
Two dedicated plugin agents with direct `subagent_type` dispatch. The RED test writer (sonnet) enforces behavioral assertions over change-detector patterns. On failure, a RED test evaluator (opus) triages with REVISE/REJECT/CONFIRM verdicts, with max one REVISE cycle before user escalation. Orchestrator integration in sprint and fix-bug only; tdd-workflow deferred pending evaluation of that skill's future.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Adversarial review: 7 findings, 7 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-d1df152a17dbcb2c/adversarial-review-83fc-81fb.json
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 5 stories closed, 10 batches executed. Validation: docs-only changes, all tests pass, no RED markers remain.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
